evals.prompt: My DSPy program is giving wrong answers and I can't figure out why. How do I debug what's being sent to the model?
evals.expected_output: Use dspy.inspect_history(n=1) immediately after running the failing input. This shows the full prompt the LM received, the raw response, and which adapter formatted the request. The debugging workflow is: run on failing input, call inspect_history(n=1), check if instructions are clear, check the raw response format.

assertions.name: uses_inspect_history
assertions.description: Recommends dspy.inspect_history(n=1) as the first debugging step, not print statements or logging

assertions.name: shows_debugging_workflow
assertions.description: Explains what inspect_history shows (prompt, raw response, adapter) not just that it exists

assertions.name: does_not_suggest_only_logging
assertions.description: Does not recommend generic Python logging as the primary debugging approach -- inspect_history is DSPy-specific

evals.prompt: I optimized my DSPy program with MIPROv2 and want to save it for production. How do I save and load it correctly?
evals.expected_output: After optimization call optimized.save('path.json') to save learned state (demos, instructions). In production create a fresh MyProgram() instance then call program.load('path.json'). Critically: call dspy.configure(lm=lm) BEFORE load(), not after. The class definition must exist in production code -- forward() logic is not saved. Use save_program=True if you want to avoid recreating the class at load time, then use dspy.load() instead of program.load().

assertions.name: configure_before_load
assertions.description: Explicitly states dspy.configure(lm=lm) must be called BEFORE program.load(), not after

assertions.name: mentions_class_required
assertions.description: Notes that the class definition (forward() logic) must exist at load time -- it is not saved in the JSON

assertions.name: mentions_dspy_load_option
assertions.description: Mentions dspy.load() with save_program=True as an alternative that avoids recreating the class

evals.prompt: I need to generate diverse training examples with DSPy but I keep getting the same cached response. How do I disable the cache for data generation?
evals.expected_output: Disable both disk and memory cache with dspy.configure_cache(enable_disk_cache=False, enable_memory_cache=False) before generating data. Or use dspy.LM('model', cache=False) per-LM. Re-enable after generation. Important: do not disable cache globally if you are also running optimizer -- optimizers depend on cache.

assertions.name: uses_correct_configure_cache_api
assertions.description: Uses dspy.configure_cache(enable_disk_cache=False, enable_memory_cache=False) -- NOT the old dspy.configure_cache(enable=False) which does not exist

assertions.name: warns_about_optimizer_cache
assertions.description: Warns that disabling cache globally breaks optimizer runs -- should only disable for specific generation steps

assertions.name: mentions_per_lm_option
assertions.description: Mentions per-LM cache control (dspy.LM('model', cache=False)) as an alternative to global disable
